message:
chore(fuzz): silence shared-module dead-code and lint warnings

- arbitrary_graph.rs: module-level ``#![allow(dead_code)]``. This
  shared module is included by ~14 fuzz targets, each of which uses a
  different subset of the generators, so each binary legitimately has
  "unused struct/function" warnings — blanket-allow at the source.
- arbitrary_graph.rs: switch manual ``n % 4 != 0`` / ``n % 8 == 0``
  multiplicity checks to ``n.is_multiple_of(...)`` per the new
  clippy::manual_is_multiple_of lint.
- fuzz_community.rs: ``node_count.clamp(1, 4)`` replaces the
  equivalent ``.min(4).max(1)`` pattern (clippy::manual_clamp).
- fuzz_matching.rs: annotate ``MatchingInput`` with
  ``#[allow(clippy::enum_variant_names)]`` — every variant ends in
  ``Matching`` because the enum is literally a dispatch over matching
  kinds; the postfix lint is counterproductive here.
- fuzz_dag.rs: drop unused ``ArbitraryDiGraph`` import.

Fuzz crate now builds and clippies clean under
``CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_networkx_cc_fuzz cargo
clippy --all-targets``.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
